description: Ingestion adapter and deduplication pipeline contract.
ingestion_run_rule: A workspace ingestion run selects enabled workspace_source_links, executes the registered adapters, persists raw_items idempotently, and records run-level source success/failure plus raw created/updated counts. Schedulers and workers must call this same service layer instead of bypassing adapters.
ingestion_empty_run_rule: limit must be null or >= 1. limit=0 is rejected by API validation. If source type filters or workspace links select zero enabled sources, the run is persisted with status no_sources, zero counters and a summary_json.hint instead of completed.
adapter_rule: Each source type may have its own input structure, but every adapter must persist the full raw payload and emit canonical raw_items. Normalization enriches and maps fields; it must not discard the original payload.
adapter_network_rule: HTTP adapters that fetch RSS, paper RSS, paper API, archive pages or sitemaps must not inherit host proxy environment variables by default; local, intranet and container-internal sources must be fetched directly unless a source-specific proxy is explicitly configured.
stub_adapter_rule: Registered placeholder adapters must not silently return zero items as a successful fetch. If a source_type is present but not implemented, the adapter must raise AdapterNotImplementedError, the source summary status must be skipped_unimplemented, and the run must record source_skipped_unimplemented without counting that source as succeeded or failed.
push_based_adapter_rule: manual and internal are implemented push-based adapters, not stubs: their entries arrive via manual-import or internal push APIs, so fetch() legitimately returns an empty list and the run records the source as succeeded with zero new items. internal upgrades to a generic JSON API puller when fetch_config.api_url is set. The stub_adapter_rule applies only to source types without an implementation.
raw_item_identity_rule: raw_items are idempotent by data_source_id + entry_key. entry_key must fit raw_items.entry_key length 255. If an upstream feed id or redirect URL is longer, the ingestion layer must deterministically shorten it with a stable hash suffix while preserving the original value inside raw_payload_json.

source_type_to_adapter.paper_api: PaperApiAdapter (arXiv v1 + OpenAlex works v1 + Semantic Scholar bulk search v1)

dedupe_stage.reason: Different source structures must first be normalized before they can be compared fairly. Scoring should only run on deduped winners.
dedupe_stage.workspace_scope_rule: dedupe_groups are unique by workspace_code + dedupe_key. Shared raw_items may be normalized independently into each enabled workspace, and each workspace owns its own winner/loser state.
dedupe_stage.dedupe_key_length_rule: dedupe_key must fit news_items.dedupe_key and dedupe_groups.dedupe_key length 512. Long canonical URLs or title/date keys are deterministically shortened with a stable hash suffix; canonical_url and raw source_url remain stored as full Text fields for lineage.
hard_dedupe_key_order: normalized_title + published_date_bucket when canonical_url is missing
canonical_url_rules: remove tracking query params such as utm_*, spm, ref, ref_src, fbclid, gclid
representative_selection_order: source_kind priority: wiseflow gets legacy bonus because it may contain richer crawled material

result_fields.loser: active = false, duplicate_of = winner.id or winner.candidate_key
result_fields.all_items: raw_items remain preserved; dedupe never deletes raw records

candidate_pool_lineage_v1.rule: Each dedupe group read model must expose a safe lineage.nodes chain for the winner: data_source -> raw_item -> news_item -> dedupe_group -> optional recommendation_item -> optional generated_news -> optional daily_report_item.
candidate_pool_lineage_v1.raw_safety_rule: The candidate pool lineage may expose raw payload keys, source URL, entry key, timestamps and content length, but must not expose the full raw_payload_json or credentials/fetch_config.
candidate_pool_lineage_v1.frontend_rule: /news must render the lineage as a reviewable trace chain, show review_note as the business meaning of each node, and support news_item_id, raw_item_id and dedupe_group_id query anchors.

recommendation_stage.current_api: POST /api/pipeline/daily-runs for full pipeline; POST /api/recommendation/runs for recommendation-only reruns; GET /api/recommendation/scorer-policy for read-only scorer policy summary; POST /api/recommendation/scorer-preview for read-only single-candidate scorer preview; POST /api/daily-reports/bulk-adopt-from-candidates and POST /api/daily-reports/bulk-reject-from-candidates for observation-pool review write-through
recommendation_stage.candidate_rule: Only active dedupe winners from the requested workspace and target day_key can enter a recommendation run. Losers remain traceable but are not recommended directly.
recommendation_stage.day_key_rule: Daily recommendation filters candidates by (news_items.published_at or created_at) in Asia/Shanghai. Freshness scoring for backfill uses the target day end instead of the wall-clock current day.
recommendation_stage.planning_intel_priority_rule: The default planning_intel scorer is technical-intelligence-first and uses a content admission layer before daily selection. It boosts AI software, AI infrastructure, model engineering, inference/training, RAG, multi-agent, agent memory, hardware vendor technology, competitor technical roadmaps, AI chips, GPU clusters, data center architecture and telecom system signals. Source-level tags are only weak priors: a vendor or hardware source is not enough by itself; the item must contain concrete technical, architecture, AI, chip, data-center or telecom evidence. It demotes finance, funding, earnings, procurement/bidding, consumer gadgets, event promotion, marketing launch events, generic partnership, pure marketing, legal/meta discussion, clickbait, social/education off-scope items, aerospace/rocket off-scope items and off-scope academic/biomedical papers unless editors explicitly override.

content_admission_levels.P0: major insight, may affect technical roadmap, architecture, cost, standards or competitive landscape
content_admission_levels.P1: high-value technical signal, eligible for daily report
content_admission_levels.P2: medium-value observation, may enter daily when budget remains or weekly/observation pool
content_admission_levels.P3: low-value archive/search-only signal, not selected into daily by default
content_admission_levels.R: rejected or noise, not selected into daily by default

content_scorer_v2.rule: The recommendation service remains responsible for candidate selection and daily generation, but content admission must be persisted as structured fields on recommendation_items. Missing config must fall back to the existing baseline content-admission logic.

policy_summary_api.rule: This endpoint returns an operational summary only. It must not return the full keyword corpus and must not imply online editing or recompute support. Single-candidate preview is provided by POST /api/recommendation/scorer-preview.

preview_api.persistence_rule: The preview must not create recommendation_runs, recommendation_items, generated_news, daily_report_items, editorial_actions or raw/news records.
preview_api.rule: Preview reuses the same content admission scorer path as recommendation runs for a temporary candidate. It is for operational validation only and is not a config editor or batch recompute API.

selection_caps.paper_rss_daily_ratio: paper_rss selected items are capped at about 10% of the requested limit
selection_caps.pool_daily_ratio: one admission pool should not exceed about 40% of the requested limit
selection_caps.selected_levels: select P0/P1 first, then clean P2 with daily-worthy technical signal; noisy P2, P2 paper_rss, P3 and R are not selected by default

observation_review_v1.source_rule: The panel must derive reviewable items from the selected recommendation run: selected=false and admission_level in P2/P3.
observation_review_v1.write_rule: Observation review must write through the existing daily report candidate adoption/rejection APIs using dedupe_group_ids. It must not bypass recommendation_item/generated_news lineage or create daily items directly from arbitrary news_items.
observation_review_v1.readback_rule: GET /api/recommendation/runs/{id} must expose the latest linked daily_report trace for each recommendation_item so the frontend can show adoption_status and day_key after review.
observation_review_v1.known_limit: This is a review v1. Strategy editing, batch recompute impact analysis, observation-pool sorting policy, reviewer notes and production sampling remain separate enhancements.

writes: daily_reports and daily_report_items when create_daily_draft is true
recommendation_stage.lineage_rule: generated_news.content_json.source must preserve news_item_id, raw_item_id and data_source_id so adopted daily report items can be traced back to raw_items.raw_payload_json.
recommendation_stage.adoption_rule: Recommendation creates draft daily_report_items with adoption_status = 2 for selected items. Editors may later change adoption_status on daily_report_items; news_items and raw_items are never overwritten. Candidate-pool bulk adoption must create or update daily_report_items from existing recommendation_items/generated_news only; candidates without a recommendation chain are skipped instead of bypassing recommendation. Candidate-pool bulk reject also requires an existing recommendation_item, writes or updates a daily_report_item with adoption_status = 0, and may create a generated_news placeholder with generation_status = rejected_candidate so rejected candidates remain traceable but cannot enter standard Company SQL export.
recommendation_stage.workspace_scope_rule: Daily reports are unique by workspace_code + domain_code + day_key so multiple workspaces can generate same-day reports independently.

weekly_report_stage.source_rule: Weekly draft generation starts from daily_report_items with adoption_status=2 in the requested ISO week, published daily reports by default, and keeps daily_report_item_id plus generated_news_id for lineage.
weekly_report_stage.adoption_rule: Generated weekly_report_items start with adoption_status=1 as weekly candidates. Admins set adoption_status=2 for final weekly adoption or 0 to remove from the weekly report.
weekly_report_stage.workspace_scope_rule: Weekly reports are unique by workspace_code + domain_code + week_key so multiple workspaces can build the same ISO week independently.

generator_stage.current_provider: MiniMax China OpenAI-compatible chat/completions at MINIMAX_BASE_URL or default https://api.minimaxi.com/v1 when MINIMAX_GENERATION_ENABLED=true and MINIMAX_API_KEY is set; deterministic rule_v1 fallback otherwise
generator_stage.provider_rule: Model output must be strict JSON, must be Simplified Chinese except for proper nouns, and category must be one of the workspace label policy primary labels. Source trace IDs are forced by backend after generation.
generator_stage.news_format_rule: For planning_intel/company_sql_v1, generated_news.content_json must contain background, effects, eventSummary, technologyAndInnovation and valueAndImpact so later SQL export can map without guessing.
generator_stage.fallback_rule: LLM timeout or failure must not abort the daily pipeline; selected recommendations still generate rule_v1:fallback drafts with the same required content_json fields, but generation_status must be fallback_needs_review and standard Company SQL export must reject them until an LLM-ready or edited-ready version exists.

latest_live_acceptance.status: passed with MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed; unsupported numeric claims not present in source are rejected by the validator

generator_stage.rerun_api: POST /api/daily-reports/{report_id}/regenerate-generated-news can rerun non-ready or selected generated_news rows for a draft daily report.

scheduled_daily_pipeline.schedule_rule: When INGESTION_SCHEDULER_DAILY_TIME is set, the scheduler must wait for that HH:MM or HH:MM:SS wall-clock time in INGESTION_SCHEDULER_TIMEZONE instead of running immediately on container start. If DAILY_PIPELINE_DAY_OFFSET_DAYS is non-zero, the scheduler must pass the derived day_key to the daily pipeline; for a morning planning_intel job use -1 so a 09:00 Asia/Shanghai run generates yesterday's daily report.
scheduled_daily_pipeline.rule: The scheduled job must call the same service functions as the manual APIs. It must not bypass adapter, normalization, dedupe or recommendation contracts.

historical_backfill_stage.purpose: Recover target-date raw/news candidates that cannot be restored from current RSS windows.
historical_backfill_stage.run_record: Reuse ingestion_runs with run_type=historical_backfill and explicit params_json.target_day_start, target_day_end, backfill_mode, source_scope, retry_policy, include_undated and manual_items count.
historical_backfill_stage.current_behavior: Fetch enabled workspace sources through the selected backfill mode, persist only raw inputs whose published_at falls inside the target day range by default, and record fetched/in-range/out-of-range/missing-date counts in summary_json.
historical_backfill_stage.mode_caveat: paper_api currently supports arXiv submittedDate date-window queries, OpenAlex works publication_date date-window queries, and Semantic Scholar publicationDateOrYear date-window queries through PaperApiAdapter. Semantic Scholar can use optional SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY for higher rate limits. OpenReview/Hugging Face Papers providers remain future adapters. rss_window is not a full archive guarantee. archive_page and sitemap are lightweight URL discovery modes, not a universal crawler.
mode_priority: rss_window: low-cost current-window recovery for target dates still visible in feeds; not a complete historical guarantee

paper_api: arXiv v1 submittedDate, OpenAlex works v1 publication_date, and Semantic Scholar bulk search v1 publicationDateOrYear date-window queries; OpenReview/Hugging Face Papers remain future providers
archive_page: vendor blogs, standard organizations, cloud/hardware archives and paginated lists
sitemap: discover dated article URLs from official sitemaps
retry_failed: low-concurrency long-timeout retry for failed sources
manual_import: CSV/SQL/manual raw import with full raw_payload_json lineage

manual_import_contract.accepted_input: manual_items supplied by API or produced by the /ingestion-runs manual import preview. Each accepted row must include data_source_id or source_id that belongs to an enabled source selected by the run, and at least one of source_title/title, source_url/url, or raw_content/content/summary.
manual_import_contract.lineage_rule: Persist imported rows as raw_items first. raw_payload_json.backfill_mode must be manual_import and raw_payload_json.payload must keep the original row payload.
manual_import_contract.current_ui: The frontend supports selecting one enabled source, uploading or pasting CSV/SQL text, previewing accepted/rejected rows, downloading the row-level error report, and only then submitting accepted rows to POST /api/ingestion/backfill-runs.
manual_import_contract.empty_success_rule: Empty manual_items, rows without source id, rows outside the selected enabled source set, rows without content, and previews with zero accepted rows must be rejected before creating a success-looking run.
manual_import_contract.error_report_rule: Preview returns row-level errors and error_report_csv; frontend must expose the report instead of collapsing invalid rows into a generic failure.

success_metrics: active winners created or replaced after dedupe
recommendation candidates and selected daily report items added
historical_backfill_stage.rule: Backfill output must enter raw_items first and then reuse the same normalization, dedupe, recommendation and SQL export pipeline. It must not insert generated_news or SQL rows directly.

failed_source_retry_stage.selection_rule: Extract only summary_json.sources entries whose status is failed. Do not retry completed sources and do not create a new run when the original run has no failed source.
failed_source_retry_stage.scope_rule: Retryable sources must still be enabled both in data_sources and in the original run workspace_source_links. Disabled or unlinked sources return a conflict instead of producing a zero-source success run.
failed_source_retry_stage.execution_rule: Retry reuses the same run service as normal ingestion/backfill with source_ids restricted to the failed source ids, default concurrency=2 and source_timeout_seconds=60. workspace_fetch preserves max_items_per_source; historical_backfill preserves target_day_start, target_day_end, backfill_mode and include_undated.
failed_source_retry_stage.lineage_rule: The retry run must record params_json.retry_of_run_id and params_json.source_ids so later coverage/debugging can trace it back to the failed source run.

auto_retry.selection_rule: Select base ingestion/backfill runs with failed sources, no retry_of_run_id, non-manual_import mode, attempt_count < max attempts, and last attempt + exponential backoff <= now. If the latest retry run has source_failed=0, the base run is considered resolved.
auto_retry.scheduler_rule: When capability_ingestion and INGESTION_FAILED_SOURCE_AUTO_RETRY_ENABLED are true, scheduler enqueues due failed-source retries through the same ingestion run services.

failed_source_retry_stage.known_limit: manual_import runs cannot be reconstructed from summary_json and must be retried by re-uploading manual import data. Alert delivery remains separate operations work.

coverage_trend_stage.source_of_truth: ingestion_runs plus summary_json.sources; no derived table is required for v1
coverage_trend_stage.time_bucket_rule: Bucket runs by completed_at, started_at or created_at in Asia/Shanghai date keys.
returns: top_failed_sources with last error and last failed run
coverage_trend_stage.frontend_rule: /ingestion-runs must render coverage trends as a diagnostic panel, not as a success banner. Zero raw_created and non-zero failures remain visible.

soft_clustering_stage.purpose: Group same-topic items across different URLs without automatically deleting them.
soft_clustering_stage.rule: Soft clusters support editor review and weekly aggregation; they do not replace hard dedupe in phase 1.
